diagnostic.message: unsupported floating expression aggregate at narrowing
floating divisor is not provably nonzero
notes: division by a value that may be 0.0 can produce infinity or NaN.

diagnostic.message: floating expression may be infinite at narrowing
floating expression may be NaN at narrowing
floating expression is not provably within target range
floating expression is not provably initialized at narrowing
current proof surface cannot derive a supported Loop_Variant from this while condition
notes: supported while-loop proof shapes are structural `Cursor != null` traversal and simple integer-bound `Lo < Hi` / `Lo <= Hi` conditions.
rewrite the loop to match one of those forms, or use a different construct whose termination proof does not depend on an emitted Loop_Variant.
PR10.2 closes the live accepted Rule 5 surface by merging the PR07 Rule 5 positives with the frozen PR10 Rule 5 emitted representative.
Rule 5 remains a narrowing-point proof boundary: IEEE 754 intermediate NaN and infinity are allowed, but narrowing points must be provably safe.
While-loop conditions outside the current derivable Loop_Variant surface are rejected during safec check rather than deferred to emitted GNATprove failure.
